Name the three basic programming constru: What is sequence?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Instructions carried out one after another, in the order written; order matters.

Name the three basic programming constru: Difference between count-controlled and condition-controlled iteration?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Count-controlled repeats a fixed, known number of times (for); condition-controlled repeats until a condition changes (while / do...until).

Name the three basic programming constru: Difference between a while loop and a do...until loop?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: while tests the condition at the start (may run zero times); do...until tests at the end (always runs at least once).

Name the three basic programming constru: Which loop is best for input validation and why?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: do...until, because you must ask for input at least once before you can test it.

Name the three basic programming constru: What is recursion?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: A subroutine (function or procedure) that calls itself to solve a problem.

Name the three basic programming constru: What two parts must every correct recursive subroutine have?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: A base case (stops the recursion) and a general/recursive case (calls itself with a value closer to the base case).

Name the three basic programming constru: What happens if a recursive routine has no reachable base case?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: It calls itself indefinitely, filling the call stack until memory runs out — a stack overflow.

Name the three basic programming constru: How is the call stack used in recursion?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Each call pushes a stack frame (parameters, local variables, return address); frames are popped in reverse (LIFO) order as calls return.

Name the three basic programming constru: Give two differences between recursion and iteration.
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Recursion uses more memory (a frame per call) and is generally slower; iteration uses one set of variables and is generally faster.

Name the three basic programming constru: What is variable scope?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: The region of a program in which a variable can be accessed (is visible).

Name the three basic programming constru: What is a local variable?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: A variable declared inside a subroutine, accessible only within it and existing only while it runs.

Name the three basic programming constru: What is a global variable?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: A variable declared in the main program, accessible (and changeable) anywhere in the program.

Name the three basic programming constru: Give two reasons to prefer local variables.
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Fewer side effects / accidental changes, memory freed when the subroutine ends, more modular and easier to debug (any two).

Name the three basic programming constru: What is modularity and give one benefit.
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Breaking a large program into smaller self-contained subroutines, each doing one task; benefit: easier to write/test/debug, reuse, or team parallel work.

Name the three basic programming constru: Difference between a function and a procedure?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: A function returns a (single) value; a procedure performs a task but returns no value.

Name the three basic programming constru: Difference between a parameter and an argument?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: A parameter is the variable in the subroutine's definition; an argument is the actual value supplied when it is called.

Name the three basic programming constru: What does passing by value mean?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: A copy of the argument's value is passed; changes inside the subroutine do not affect the original variable.

Name the three basic programming constru: What does passing by reference mean?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: The memory address of the original variable is passed; changes inside the subroutine do affect the original.

Name the three basic programming constru: Give one advantage each of by value and by reference.
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: By value: safer, avoids unintended side effects. By reference: efficient for large data (no copy) and can return changes through the parameter.

Name the three basic programming constru: What is an IDE?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Integrated Development Environment — one application giving the tools to write, test and debug code.

Name the three basic programming constru: Name three IDE debugging features.
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Breakpoints (pause at a line), stepping (run one line at a time), variable watch/watch window (inspect values at run time).

Name the three basic programming constru: Name two IDE editing features that speed up writing code.
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Syntax highlighting, code completion/auto-complete (intellisense), auto-indentation/pretty-printing, error highlighting (any two).

Name the three basic programming constru: What is a class and what is an object?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: A class is a template/blueprint defining attributes and methods; an object is a specific instance of a class created at run time.

Name the three basic programming constru: Difference between an attribute and a method?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: An attribute is a data item (variable) belonging to an object; a method is a subroutine (behaviour) belonging to a class.

Name the three basic programming constru: What is a constructor?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: A special method that runs when an object is created, used to initialise its attributes (often 'new' in OCR ERL).

Name the three basic programming constru: What is encapsulation?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Bundling attributes and methods inside a class and keeping attributes private, accessed only through public methods (data hiding).

Name the three basic programming constru: What are getters and setters?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Public methods that read (getter/accessor) and write (setter/mutator) private attributes in a controlled way; a setter can validate first.

Name the three basic programming constru: What is inheritance?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: A subclass (child) acquiring the attributes and methods of a superclass (parent), modelling an 'is-a' relationship and promoting reuse.

Name the three basic programming constru: What is polymorphism?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: The same method call behaving differently depending on the object's class; most commonly achieved by overriding a method in a subclass.

Name the three basic programming constru: Difference between overriding and overloading?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Overriding: a subclass replaces an inherited method with the same name and parameters. Overloading: several methods share a name but have different parameter lists.

Name the three basic programming constru: List three features that make a problem solvable by computational methods.
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Can be decomposed into sub-problems, has clear inputs/processes/outputs, is repetitive/rule-based, useful abstractions can be made, data can be structured (any three).

Name the three basic programming constru: What is decomposition?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Breaking a complex problem into smaller, more manageable sub-problems that can each be solved and tested individually.

Name the three basic programming constru: What is divide and conquer?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Repeatedly dividing a problem into parts, solving each part, then combining the results (e.g. binary search, merge sort).

Name the three basic programming constru: Difference between decomposition and divide and conquer?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Decomposition breaks a problem into different sub-tasks; divide and conquer breaks it into smaller instances of the same problem and combines their solutions.

Name the three basic programming constru: What is abstraction?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Removing or hiding unnecessary detail so you focus only on the information needed to solve the problem.

Name the three basic programming constru: Name the two types of abstraction.
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Representational abstraction (remove unneeded detail, e.g. Tube map) and abstraction by generalisation (group by shared characteristics).

Name the three basic programming constru: What is backtracking and where is it used?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Building a solution step by step and reversing on dead ends to try alternatives; used in mazes, N-Queens, Sudoku and pathfinding.

Name the three basic programming constru: What is data mining and where is it used?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Searching large data sets to discover hidden patterns/trends; used in supermarket loyalty-card analysis and fraud detection.

Name the three basic programming constru: What is a heuristic and where is it used?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: A rule-of-thumb giving a good-enough (not guaranteed optimal) answer quickly; used in satnav routing (A*) and the Travelling Salesman Problem.

Name the three basic programming constru: What is performance modelling and where is it used?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Using a mathematical/computational model to predict how a system will perform instead of testing the real thing; used for server load or aircraft systems.

Name the three basic programming constru: What is pipelining and where is it used?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Splitting a process into stages so different items are processed at the same time; used in the CPU fetch-decode-execute cycle.

Name the three basic programming constru: What is visualisation and where is it used?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Representing data graphically (charts, maps, heat maps) to reveal patterns for humans; used for sales trends or website click heat maps.

Name the three basic programming constru: Apply: a satnav must route across a huge road network where the exact route is too slow to compute. Which method and why?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: A heuristic — it finds a good-enough route quickly because computing the optimal route is intractable for a large network.

Name the three basic programming constru: Apply: trace factorial(3) where factorial(n)=n*factorial(n-1), base case n==0 returns 1.
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: factorial(0)=1, factorial(1)=1, factorial(2)=2, factorial(3)=6.

Name the three basic programming constru: Apply: why does deep recursion risk a stack overflow?
Sequence, selection (branching) and iter: Each active call adds a frame to the call stack; too many simultaneous frames exhaust the available memory.
